title: Google Is Censoring Reviews of ICE Detention Centers
excerpt: In the past year, Google Maps was deleting all the reviews for dozens of ICE jails, a new study found.
content_html:
While they’re not as common as reviews for cafes and dry cleaners, reviews have poured into Google Maps by the thousands for the growing network of jails operated by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The reviews have come from internet users sharing their often harrowing experiences in the detention facilities.

Google then purges these reviews and blocks the ability to write new ones, according to newly published research.

“Google actively suppresses carceral information, narratives, and digital trace data related to the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s (ICE) detention facilities,” wrote Muira McCammon, a professor at Tulane University’s Department of Communication who has been scraping and analyzing self-reported accounts of ICE imprisonment posted to Google Maps since last year.

Much of this record is now permanently deleted, erasing detailed testimony of people ingested by the U.S. deportation machine.

Last August, McCammon aggregated 1,636 posts from 26 different ICE facilities where Google still allowed users to post reviews and discovered there were 91 other immigration jails on Google Maps with no reviews.

“Google had already turned off reviews for the remaining 91 facilities,” McCammon told The Intercept. “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”

Only four ICE facilities appear to still permit reviews on Google Maps, she said.

Pulling the reviews has come with no public notice or explanation.

In 2023, Google said in a corporate blog post that it reserved the right to delete Maps reviews for locations that are “consistently unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic. This includes places that people go to without choice or places only accessible to people stationed or assigned there—such as police stations and prisons.”

Unhelpful to whom, however, is unclear: While some of the reviews in question were statements of protest that could have been left by anyone, many appeared to come from people directly affected by ICE. They described a variety of issues that would be of great material interest to anyone detained in the facilities or for people close to them.

“Reviews help people make decisions on where to go,” Google spokesperson Genevieve Park said in a statement, “and we don’t show reviews on several categories of places where people go without choice,” citing the company’s policy of removing Google Maps reviews that are “unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic.”

In the past, Google has complied with requests to censor speech about ICE. In 2025, the company blocked Android users from downloading ICEBlock, an app that alerted people to crowdsourced sightings of ICE agents.

Asked if the Department of Homeland Security, ICE’s parent agency, had communicated with Google about the reviews, Park said the takedowns were “unrelated—it was about enforcing our policy that prohibits reviews for places that people go to without choice.”

In a statement to The Intercept, a spokesperson for DHS denied that ICE requested any reviews of detention facilities be removed.

“Being in detention is a choice,” the Homeland Security spokesperson said, citing the department’s program to encourage an app-based system for self-deportation.

Many of the reviews McCammon aggregated were from despairing friends and relatives.

“My friend’s dad was sentenced to one month in jail,” read one review. “He served the time and then was moved to this facility for another 3 months. How is it not illegal to hold someone 3x longer than what they were sentenced to serve?”

A Spanish language review McCammon collected read, “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”

Other posts reflect what apparent former detainees described as poor conditions, such as “The place is infested with roaches crawling all over the inmates body there,” or “Very bad experience i spent 2 months in this hell . . . 99% of staff is cruel and racist.”

One reviewer said many detainees weren’t able to communicate with ICE staff because of the language barrier:

I stayed there for more than a month. The food there was okay, but very unpalatable. They were all very kind. Due to the language barrier, there was a big barrier to communication. I felt that most people couldn’t express their thoughts and needs. Why didn’t they have a translator? ! Even if it’s not expensive. The air conditioner there was very cold, and many people, including me, caught cold from the cold. Even so, they were unwilling to turn up the air conditioner temperature. When I slept at night, I only had a thin blanket to cover myself, and it felt very cold.

Although Google’s stance appears to be that the censorship is based on a common-sense, consumer-centric view of Google Maps’ purpose, McCammon argues in her paper that these are essentially political decisions, made by “suppressing the circulation of carceral information and counter-narratives [and] calling into question how the company approaches the ideology of search, when it concerns the reputation of the U.S. administrative state.”

blocks.html_en: While they’re not as common as reviews for cafes and dry cleaners, reviews have poured into Google Maps by the thousands for the growing network of jails operated by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The reviews have come from internet users sharing their often harrowing experiences in the detention facilities.

sentences.en: While they’re not as common as reviews for cafes and dry cleaners, reviews have poured into Google Maps by the thousands for the growing network of jails operated by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
sentences.zh: 虽然它们不像咖啡馆和干洗店的评论那么常见，但针对美国移民和海关执法局运营的不断增长的监狱网络，谷歌地图上已经有数千条评论涌入。

sentences.en: The reviews have come from internet users sharing their often harrowing experiences in the detention facilities.
sentences.zh: 这些评论来自互联网用户，他们分享了他们在拘留设施中经常遭受的痛苦经历。

blocks.html_en: Google then purges these reviews and blocks the ability to write new ones, according to newly published research.

sentences.en: Google then purges these reviews and blocks the ability to write new ones, according to newly published research.
sentences.zh: 根据新发表的研究，谷歌随后会清除这些评论并阻止撰写新评论的能力。

blocks.html_en: “Google actively suppresses carceral information, narratives, and digital trace data related to the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s (ICE) detention facilities,” wrote Muira McCammon, a professor at Tulane University’s Department of Communication who has been scraping and analyzing self-reported accounts of ICE imprisonment posted to Google Maps since last year.

sentences.en: “Google actively suppresses carceral information, narratives, and digital trace data related to the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s (ICE) detention facilities,” wrote Muira McCammon, a professor at Tulane University’s Department of Communication who has been scraping and analyzing self-reported accounts of ICE imprisonment posted to Google Maps since last year.
sentences.zh: 杜兰大学传播系教授 Muira McCammon 写道：“谷歌积极压制与美国移民和海关执法局 (ICE) 拘留设施相关的监禁信息、叙述和数字追踪数据。”自去年以来，她一直在抓取和分析发布在谷歌地图上的 ICE 监禁自我报告账户。

blocks.html_en: Much of this record is now permanently deleted, erasing detailed testimony of people ingested by the U.S. deportation machine.

sentences.en: Much of this record is now permanently deleted, erasing detailed testimony of people ingested by the U.S. deportation machine.
sentences.zh: 现在，大部分记录已被永久删除，从而抹去了被美国驱逐机吞下的人的详细证词。

blocks.html_en: “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”

sentences.en: “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”
sentences.zh: “我最初在 2025 年收集的几乎所有 Google 评论现在都不再可用。”

blocks.html_en: “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”

sentences.en: “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”
sentences.zh: “我最初在 2025 年收集的几乎所有 Google 评论现在都不再可用。”

blocks.html_en: Last August, McCammon aggregated 1,636 posts from 26 different ICE facilities where Google still allowed users to post reviews and discovered there were 91 other immigration jails on Google Maps with no reviews.

sentences.en: Last August, McCammon aggregated 1,636 posts from 26 different ICE facilities where Google still allowed users to post reviews and discovered there were 91 other immigration jails on Google Maps with no reviews.
sentences.zh: 去年 8 月，麦卡蒙汇总了来自 26 个不同 ICE 设施的 1,636 个帖子，谷歌仍然允许用户在这些设施中发表评论，并发现谷歌地图上还有其他 91 个移民监狱没有评论。

blocks.html_en: “Google had already turned off reviews for the remaining 91 facilities,” McCammon told The Intercept. “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”

sentences.en: “Google had already turned off reviews for the remaining 91 facilities,” McCammon told The Intercept.
sentences.zh: “谷歌已经关闭了对其余 91 个设施的审查，”麦卡蒙告诉 The Intercept。

sentences.en: “Almost all of the Google reviews that I originally collected in 2025 are now no longer available.”
sentences.zh: “我最初在 2025 年收集的几乎所有 Google 评论现在都不再可用。”

blocks.html_en: Only four ICE facilities appear to still permit reviews on Google Maps, she said.

sentences.en: Only four ICE facilities appear to still permit reviews on Google Maps, she said.
sentences.zh: 她说，似乎只有四家 ICE 设施仍然允许在谷歌地图上进行审查。

blocks.html_en: Pulling the reviews has come with no public notice or explanation.

sentences.en: Pulling the reviews has come with no public notice or explanation.
sentences.zh: 撤回评论是在没有公开通知或解释的情况下进行的。

blocks.html_en: In 2023, Google said in a corporate blog post that it reserved the right to delete Maps reviews for locations that are “consistently unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic. This includes places that people go to without choice or places only accessible to people stationed or assigned there—such as police stations and prisons.”

sentences.en: In 2023, Google said in a corporate blog post that it reserved the right to delete Maps reviews for locations that are “consistently unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic.
sentences.zh: 2023 年，谷歌在一篇公司博客文章中表示，它保留删除“始终无益、有害或偏离主题的地点的地图评论”的权利。

sentences.en: This includes places that people go to without choice or places only accessible to people stationed or assigned there—such as police stations and prisons.”
sentences.zh: 这包括人们没有选择去的地方，或者只有驻扎或分配在那里的人才能进入的地方，例如警察局和监狱。”

blocks.html_en: Unhelpful to whom, however, is unclear: While some of the reviews in question were statements of protest that could have been left by anyone, many appeared to come from people directly affected by ICE. They described a variety of issues that would be of great material interest to anyone detained in the facilities or for people close to them.

sentences.en: Unhelpful to whom, however, is unclear: While some of the reviews in question were statements of protest that could have been left by anyone, many appeared to come from people directly affected by ICE.
sentences.zh: 然而，尚不清楚对谁没有帮助：虽然一些评论是任何人都可以留下的抗议声明，但许多评论似乎来自直接受到 ICE 影响的人。

sentences.en: They described a variety of issues that would be of great material interest to anyone detained in the facilities or for people close to them.
sentences.zh: 他们描述了各种问题，这些问题对于被拘留在设施中的任何人或与他们关系密切的人来说都具有极大的物质利益。

blocks.html_en: “Reviews help people make decisions on where to go,” Google spokesperson Genevieve Park said in a statement, “and we don’t show reviews on several categories of places where people go without choice,” citing the company’s policy of removing Google Maps reviews that are “unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic.”

sentences.en: “Reviews help people make decisions on where to go,” Google spokesperson Genevieve Park said in a statement, “and we don’t show reviews on several categories of places where people go without choice,” citing the company’s policy of removing Google Maps reviews that are “unhelpful, harmful, or off-topic.”
sentences.zh: 谷歌发言人吉纳维夫·帕克(Genevieve Park)在一份声明中表示，“评论可以帮助人们决定去哪里，我们不会显示人们别无选择的几类地方的评论”，并援引了该公司删除“无益、有害或偏离主题”的谷歌地图评论的政策。

blocks.html_en: In the past, Google has complied with requests to censor speech about ICE. In 2025, the company blocked Android users from downloading ICEBlock, an app that alerted people to crowdsourced sightings of ICE agents.

sentences.en: In the past, Google has complied with requests to censor speech about ICE.
sentences.zh: 过去，谷歌曾遵守审查有关 ICE 言论的要求。

sentences.en: In 2025, the company blocked Android users from downloading ICEBlock, an app that alerted people to crowdsourced sightings of ICE agents.
sentences.zh: 2025 年，该公司阻止 Android 用户下载 ICEBlock，这是一款提醒人们众包目击 ICE 特工的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: Asked if the Department of Homeland Security, ICE’s parent agency, had communicated with Google about the reviews, Park said the takedowns were “unrelated—it was about enforcing our policy that prohibits reviews for places that people go to without choice.”

sentences.en: Asked if the Department of Homeland Security, ICE’s parent agency, had communicated with Google about the reviews, Park said the takedowns were “unrelated—it was about enforcing our policy that prohibits reviews for places that people go to without choice.”
sentences.zh: 当被问及 ICE 的上级机构国土安全部是否就这些评论与谷歌进行过沟通时，帕克表示，这些删除行为“无关紧要——这是为了执行我们的政策，禁止对人们没有选择的地方进行评论。”

blocks.html_en: In a statement to The Intercept, a spokesperson for DHS denied that ICE requested any reviews of detention facilities be removed.

sentences.en: In a statement to The Intercept, a spokesperson for DHS denied that ICE requested any reviews of detention facilities be removed.
sentences.zh: 在给 The Intercept 的一份声明中，国土安全部发言人否认 ICE 要求取消对拘留设施的任何审查。

blocks.html_en: “Being in detention is a choice,” the Homeland Security spokesperson said, citing the department’s program to encourage an app-based system for self-deportation.

sentences.en: “Being in detention is a choice,” the Homeland Security spokesperson said, citing the department’s program to encourage an app-based system for self-deportation.
sentences.zh: “被拘留是一种选择，”国土安全部发言人说，并引用了该部门鼓励基于应用程序的自我驱逐系统的计划。

blocks.html_en: Many of the reviews McCammon aggregated were from despairing friends and relatives.

sentences.en: Many of the reviews McCammon aggregated were from despairing friends and relatives.
sentences.zh: 麦卡蒙收集的许多评论来自绝望的朋友和亲戚。

blocks.html_en: “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”

sentences.en: “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”
sentences.zh: “打电话时你几乎听不到被拘留者的声音。”

blocks.html_en: “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”

sentences.en: “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”
sentences.zh: “打电话时你几乎听不到被拘留者的声音。”

blocks.html_en: “My friend’s dad was sentenced to one month in jail,” read one review. “He served the time and then was moved to this facility for another 3 months. How is it not illegal to hold someone 3x longer than what they were sentenced to serve?”

sentences.en: “My friend’s dad was sentenced to one month in jail,” read one review.
sentences.zh: “我朋友的父亲被判入狱一个月，”一篇评论写道。

sentences.en: “He served the time and then was moved to this facility for another 3 months.
sentences.zh: “他服刑完毕后，又被转移到这个设施里又待了 3 个月。

sentences.en: How is it not illegal to hold someone 3x longer than what they were sentenced to serve?”
sentences.zh: 关押某人的时间比其被判处的刑期长三倍怎么不违法呢？”

blocks.html_en: A Spanish language review McCammon collected read, “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”

sentences.en: A Spanish language review McCammon collected read, “You can barely hear the detainee when you make a phone call.”
sentences.zh: 麦卡蒙收集的一篇西班牙语评论写道：“打电话时你几乎听不到被拘留者的声音。”

blocks.html_en: Other posts reflect what apparent former detainees described as poor conditions, such as “The place is infested with roaches crawling all over the inmates body there,” or “Very bad experience i spent 2 months in this hell . . . 99% of staff is cruel and racist.”

sentences.en: Other posts reflect what apparent former detainees described as poor conditions, such as “The place is infested with roaches crawling all over the inmates body there,” or “Very bad experience i spent 2 months in this hell . . .
sentences.zh: 其他帖子反映了明显的前被拘留者所描述的恶劣条件，例如“这个地方到处都是蟑螂，在囚犯身上爬行”，或者“我在这个地狱里度过了两个月的经历非常糟糕......

sentences.en: 99% of staff is cruel and racist.”
sentences.zh: 99% 的员工都是残忍和种族主义的。”

blocks.html_en: One reviewer said many detainees weren’t able to communicate with ICE staff because of the language barrier:

sentences.en: One reviewer said many detainees weren’t able to communicate with ICE staff because of the language barrier:
sentences.zh: 一位评论员表示，由于语言障碍，许多被拘留者无法与 ICE 工作人员沟通：

blocks.html_en: I stayed there for more than a month. The food there was okay, but very unpalatable. They were all very kind. Due to the language barrier, there was a big barrier to communication. I felt that most people couldn’t express their thoughts and needs. Why didn’t they have a translator? ! Even if it’s not expensive. The air conditioner there was very cold, and many people, including me, caught cold from the cold. Even so, they were unwilling to turn up the air conditioner temperature. When I slept at night, I only had a thin blanket to cover myself, and it felt very cold.

sentences.en: I stayed there for more than a month.
sentences.zh: 我在那里呆了一个多月。

sentences.en: The food there was okay, but very unpalatable.
sentences.zh: 那里的食物还可以，但是非常难吃。

sentences.en: They were all very kind.
sentences.zh: 他们都非常友善。

sentences.en: Due to the language barrier, there was a big barrier to communication.
sentences.zh: 由于语言不通，沟通存在很大障碍。

sentences.en: I felt that most people couldn’t express their thoughts and needs.
sentences.zh: 我觉得大多数人都无法表达自己的想法和需求。

sentences.en: Why didn’t they have a translator? !
sentences.zh: 为什么他们没有翻译？ ！

sentences.en: Even if it’s not expensive.
sentences.zh: 即使它并不贵。

sentences.en: The air conditioner there was very cold, and many people, including me, caught cold from the cold.
sentences.zh: 那里的空调很冷，包括我在内的很多人都被冻得感冒了。

sentences.en: Even so, they were unwilling to turn up the air conditioner temperature.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，他们也不愿意把空调温度调高。

sentences.en: When I slept at night, I only had a thin blanket to cover myself, and it felt very cold.
sentences.zh: 晚上睡觉的时候，我只盖了一条薄被，感觉很冷。

sentences.zh: 尽管谷歌的立场似乎是，审查制度是基于对谷歌地图目的的常识性、以消费者为中心的看法，但麦卡蒙在她的论文中指出，这些本质上是政治决定，是通过“压制监狱信息和反叙事的传播[并]质疑该公司在涉及美国行政国家声誉时如何对待搜索意识形态。”

content_html_zh:
虽然它们不像咖啡馆和干洗店的评论那么常见，但针对美国移民和海关执法局运营的不断增长的监狱网络，谷歌地图上已经有数千条评论涌入。 这些评论来自互联网用户，他们分享了他们在拘留设施中经常遭受的痛苦经历。

根据新发表的研究，谷歌随后会清除这些评论并阻止撰写新评论的能力。

杜兰大学传播系教授 Muira McCammon 写道：“谷歌积极压制与美国移民和海关执法局 (ICE) 拘留设施相关的监禁信息、叙述和数字追踪数据。”自去年以来，她一直在抓取和分析发布在谷歌地图上的 ICE 监禁自我报告账户。

现在，大部分记录已被永久删除，从而抹去了被美国驱逐机吞下的人的详细证词。

去年 8 月，麦卡蒙汇总了来自 26 个不同 ICE 设施的 1,636 个帖子，谷歌仍然允许用户在这些设施中发表评论，并发现谷歌地图上还有其他 91 个移民监狱没有评论。

“谷歌已经关闭了对其余 91 个设施的审查，”麦卡蒙告诉 The Intercept。 “我最初在 2025 年收集的几乎所有 Google 评论现在都不再可用。”

她说，似乎只有四家 ICE 设施仍然允许在谷歌地图上进行审查。

撤回评论是在没有公开通知或解释的情况下进行的。

2023 年，谷歌在一篇公司博客文章中表示，它保留删除“始终无益、有害或偏离主题的地点的地图评论”的权利。 这包括人们没有选择去的地方，或者只有驻扎或分配在那里的人才能进入的地方，例如警察局和监狱。”

然而，尚不清楚对谁没有帮助：虽然一些评论是任何人都可以留下的抗议声明，但许多评论似乎来自直接受到 ICE 影响的人。 他们描述了各种问题，这些问题对于被拘留在设施中的任何人或与他们关系密切的人来说都具有极大的物质利益。

谷歌发言人吉纳维夫·帕克(Genevieve Park)在一份声明中表示，“评论可以帮助人们决定去哪里，我们不会显示人们别无选择的几类地方的评论”，并援引了该公司删除“无益、有害或偏离主题”的谷歌地图评论的政策。

过去，谷歌曾遵守审查有关 ICE 言论的要求。 2025 年，该公司阻止 Android 用户下载 ICEBlock，这是一款提醒人们众包目击 ICE 特工的应用程序。

当被问及 ICE 的上级机构国土安全部是否就这些评论与谷歌进行过沟通时，帕克表示，这些删除行为“无关紧要——这是为了执行我们的政策，禁止对人们没有选择的地方进行评论。”

在给 The Intercept 的一份声明中，国土安全部发言人否认 ICE 要求取消对拘留设施的任何审查。

“被拘留是一种选择，”国土安全部发言人说，并引用了该部门鼓励基于应用程序的自我驱逐系统的计划。

麦卡蒙收集的许多评论来自绝望的朋友和亲戚。

“我朋友的父亲被判入狱一个月，”一篇评论写道。 “他服刑完毕后，又被转移到这个设施里又待了 3 个月。 关押某人的时间比其被判处的刑期长三倍怎么不违法呢？”

麦卡蒙收集的一篇西班牙语评论写道：“打电话时你几乎听不到被拘留者的声音。”

其他帖子反映了明显的前被拘留者所描述的恶劣条件，例如“这个地方到处都是蟑螂，在囚犯身上爬行”，或者“我在这个地狱里度过了两个月的经历非常糟糕...... 99% 的员工都是残忍和种族主义的。”

一位评论员表示，由于语言障碍，许多被拘留者无法与 ICE 工作人员沟通：

我在那里呆了一个多月。 那里的食物还可以，但是非常难吃。 他们都非常友善。 由于语言不通，沟通存在很大障碍。 我觉得大多数人都无法表达自己的想法和需求。 为什么他们没有翻译？ ！ 即使它并不贵。 那里的空调很冷，包括我在内的很多人都被冻得感冒了。 即便如此，他们也不愿意把空调温度调高。 晚上睡觉的时候，我只盖了一条薄被，感觉很冷。

尽管谷歌的立场似乎是，审查制度是基于对谷歌地图目的的常识性、以消费者为中心的看法，但麦卡蒙在她的论文中指出，这些本质上是政治决定，是通过“压制监狱信息和反叙事的传播[并]质疑该公司在涉及美国行政国家声誉时如何对待搜索意识形态。”
